title: Syntax error: Bad for loop variable
body:
After a succesful compilation, I have encountered with a bash/sh syntax error during the installation.

```./src/mkinstalldirs: 9: Syntax error: Bad for loop variable```

* To solve it change the shebang of the file from `#!/bin/sh` to `#!/bin/bash`
Also change the default shell in the Makefile that is generated.
```SHELL = /bin/bash```

Then install: `sudo make install`
```line X: $':\r': command not found``` :/
* Change all line endings: `find . -type f -print0 | xargs -0 dos2unix`
Then recompile and install.

Ubuntu LTS 20.04
code_blocks:
* To solve it change the shebang of the file from `#!/bin/sh` to `#!/bin/bash`
Also change the default shell in the Makefile that is generated.
